questions.question: How many incumbents did Trump help defeat in Indiana's Republican primaries?
questions.explanation: Trump helped defeat at least five incumbents in Indiana after they had blocked his mid-decade redistricting plan.

questions.question: What unusual item did Pete Hegseth joke about when dismissing reports tied to Iran?
questions.explanation: Hegseth mocked the reports by saying he could not confirm or deny whether the U.S. has kamikaze dolphins, but could confirm they do not.

questions.question: What did Tennessee Republicans' new congressional map do to Memphis?
options: Created a new majority-Black district centered on Memphis
Left Memphis untouched but redrew Nashville
Broke up majority-Black Memphis, likely eliminating the state's only Democratic House seat
Merged Memphis with Knoxville to create a competitive district
questions.explanation: The new map broke up majority-Black Memphis and was expected to wipe out Tennessee's only Democratic U.S. House seat, giving Republicans a path to all nine seats.

questions.question: Whose office did the FBI search as part of a corruption investigation tied to possible marijuana-related bribery?
questions.explanation: The FBI searched the office of Virginia state Sen. L. Louise Lucas, who had helped lead the state's redistricting plan and had not been charged.

questions.question: What did the FBI say about reports that it had launched a criminal leak investigation into an Atlantic writer over reporting on Kash Patel?
options: It confirmed the investigation was active
It said the matter had been referred to the Justice Department
It said no such investigation exists
It declined to comment on an ongoing investigation
questions.explanation: The FBI denied the report, saying "no such investigation" exists and calling the claim completely false.

questions.question: After pausing Project Freedom, what did Trump say remained "in full force and effect"?
questions.explanation: Trump said Project Freedom was paused because of progress toward an agreement with Iran, but the U.S. blockade remained in full force and effect.

questions.question: What did the Supreme Court temporarily restore regarding mifepristone?
questions.explanation: The court temporarily restored access to mifepristone by mail and telehealth after Justice Samuel Alito Jr. paused a lower-court order restricting it.

questions.question: What share of Americans disapprove of the job Trump is doing as president?
questions.explanation: A record-high 62% of Americans disapprove of Trump's job performance across his two terms in office.

questions.question: What action did the Education Department take against Smith College?
options: It opened a Title IX investigation over the college allowing transgender women to enroll and access women's spaces and sports
It suspended all federal student aid to the college over antisemitism complaints
It fined the college for failing to report foreign donations
It revoked the college's accreditation for violating campus free speech rules
questions.explanation: The Education Department opened a Title IX investigation into Smith College, alleging it violated federal sex discrimination law by allowing transgender women to enroll and use women's housing, bathrooms, locker rooms, and sports.
